title: Empowering Frontend Developers with Serverless Computing

questions.sentence: Serverless computing is a cloud execution model that allows developers to build and run applications without the need to manage physical or virtual servers.
Instead of maintaining a server 24/7, you write 'Serverless Functions'—small, event-driven pieces of code that only run when triggered.

For a frontend developer, this is incredibly empowering as it allows you to build backend functionality, such as handling form submissions, interacting with a database, or processing payments, without needing extensive DevOps knowledge.
Major cloud providers like AWS (Lambda), Vercel (Edge Functions), and Netlify (Serverless Functions) offer simple ways to deploy these services.

While 'serverless' doesn't mean there are no servers involved, it abstracts the infrastructure management away, allowing you to focus entirely on writing feature-rich applications and reducing operational costs.
